mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This reflects a healthy economic environment where consumers are confident and spending.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth consistently correlates with negative S&P 500 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if the correlation disappears entirely (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which in turn drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as economic concerns mount.
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months but S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive in more than 60% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary environment that encourages risk-taking and boosts equity valuations by lowering discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat (absolute return < 0.5%) when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are below their specified thresholds for a sustained period (e.g., 6 consecutive months).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate healthy investment and consumer confidence, which provides a short-term boost to market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST shows strong growth during an economic expansion but S&P 500 5-day returns are consistently negative or flat (mean return < 0.1%) in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, signaling a weakening economy that will negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in the majority (> 60%) of such instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strong and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth consistently correlates with negative S&P 500 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if the correlation disappears entirely (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and retail sales (RSAFS) are also growing robustly, it indicates broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence, which provides a strong foundation for positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized and RSAFS growth > 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:strong GDP and retail sales indicate economic health
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 and RSAFS show strong growth as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently negative or flat (mean return < 0.5%) in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, an unexpected increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a more aggressive stance by the central bank, which can lead to immediate negative market reactions and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a single month during a tightening cycle, but S&P 500 5-day returns are predominantly positive (> 60%) in such instances.
